temporal_description: A close-up shot focuses on a man sitting in the driver's seat of a car. He has dark, disheveled hair and a somber expression. He is wearing a dark jacket. With his right hand, which is covered in a white, possibly bandaged sleeve, he runs his hand over his forehead in a gesture of stress or exhaustion. The background is a blurry view of the car's interior and a dimly lit, out-of-focus exterior. In the top left corner of the screen, there is a watermark with Chinese characters. The camera remains steady, maintaining a tight focus on the man's emotional state. A man speaks in a low, weary voice, filled with a sense of dread and resignation. His words are slow and deliberate, as if he's confessing a deep-seated fear. "我真是怕。你知道我一向来真格的留在最后的吗？" (I'm really scared. You know I've always saved the serious stuff for last?) The background is quiet, with no discernible music or sound effects, placing all the focus on the man's troubled monologue.
